data.title: Nasdaq-listed Oxbridge selects Coinbase Prime to support its strategic Bitcoin and Ethereum reserve program
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News: Nasdaq-listed Oxbridge Re Holdings Limited announced that it has selected Coinbase Prime to support its purchase and secure custody of Bitcoin and Ethereum and other potential cryptocurrencies as treasury reserve assets. 
Earlier, Oxbridge Re Holdings Limited announced that its board of directors had approved the decision to include Bitcoin and Ethereum in its company's reserve assets. This decision reflects its strategy of diversifying financial assets while exploring inflation-resistant assets with long-term growth potential, such as Bitcoin, Ethereum and other potential cryptocurrencies.

data.title: BNB Surpasses 580 USDT with a 1.19% Increase in 24 Hours
data.description: On Feb 06, 2025, 06:40 AM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, BNB has crossed the 580 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 580.73999 USDT, with a narrowed 1.19% increase in 24 hours.

data.title: Analysis: The average cost price of Berachain VC is $0.82. In a bull market, the price of BERA will be between $10 and $15.
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Crypto researcher and DYθR founder @hmalviya9 published an analysis on Berachain's valuation on X: "Berachain raised $82 million at an average valuation of $414 million by allocating about 34.3% of the supply to VC. The average VC entry price was about $1.207.
If BERA matches SUI's unrealized VC returns (about 15 times), the price should be above $15 at launch, but I think this possibility is low.
If the token matches SEI's unrealized VC returns (about 10 times), the price should be above $10 at launch. Therefore, BERA will be between $10-15 in a bull market and between $5-10 in a bear market."

data.title: Jupiter: Only X platform accounts were affected, customer and project funds are safe
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Jupiter officially announced that it has regained control of the X account. It should be clear that customer or project funds have never been threatened. All procedures and funds are kept in a secure multi-signature. No other communication channels have been affected. Fortunately, all members of the team are safe. Jupiter is completely safe, the problem is limited to the X account, and the situation is currently under control.

data.title: South Korean police arrest 90 drug gang members suspected of using cryptocurrencies
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News South Korean police arrested 90 criminals suspected of using cryptocurrency to sell drugs in "bars and clubs across the country". The gang members are all Vietnamese citizens.
Police said the gang "systematically smuggled drugs" into South Korea and then allegedly "distributed drugs" in bars and other clubs. Police believe that the gang brought 1.04 billion won (721,000 US dollars) worth of drugs into the country and sold 710 million won (nearly 500,000 US dollars) worth of drugs to customers in clubs and bars.
Police added that the gang used crypto assets as a payment tool to sell, smuggle and purchase drugs. (Money Today)

data.title: Raydium derivatives contracts hit $100 million in daily trading volume within weeks of launch
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Raydium began offering derivatives contracts a few weeks ago, and its daily trading volume has reached $100 million. These contracts allow cryptocurrency traders to speculate on price fluctuations without holding actual tokens.
Currently, Raydium is the third most popular perpetual contract trading platform in the Solana ecosystem, second only to Jupiter and Drift. Although Raydium perpetual contracts are still in their infancy, their growth is still strong; its creators have not invested marketing funds to promote trading tools that have not yet been officially launched. InfraRAY, a core contributor to the project, said: "The Raydium brand is still very influential."
It is worth mentioning that most traders who use the Raydium derivatives trading platform have never visited its website, but accessed its services through trading aggregators, which disperse orders to multiple venues. This means that Raydium's activities may decrease, and crucially, its relationship with direct users (traders) will also weaken. InfraRAY said: "Raydium does a good job as a market maker, but when you build relationships with takers, the network effect is even greater." In addition, Raydium perpetual contract trading is supported by the Orderly Network, which allows perpetual contract traders from multiple blockchains to trade assets on a unified order book. Perpetual contract traders on Solana now contribute 25% of Orderly's total trading volume. CEO Ran Yi said: "Among the dozens of projects that provide perpetual contract trading supported by Orderly, we have a daily trading volume of between $200 million and $400 million." (CoinDesk)
